findings.message: Unbounded full-table scan with no LIMIT: `select(Item.id)` fetches every row's id into Python memory. The existing /items endpoint caps reads at MAX_PAGE_SIZE=100; this endpoint has no ceiling, so a table with M rows loads M ids before the N+1 loop even begins.
findings.suggestion: Add the same limit/offset pagination the /items endpoint uses, or at minimum append `.limit(MAX_PAGE_SIZE)` to the scalar select so the result set is bounded server-side.

findings.message: N+1 query: `session.get(Item, iid)` inside the loop issues a separate SELECT for every id returned by the initial scan — 1 + N round-trips total, documented explicitly in the endpoint's own docstring. On an unbounded table this degrades linearly and can exhaust the connection pool under concurrency.
findings.suggestion: Replace both steps with a single projection query: `list(session.scalars(select(Item.name).order_by(Item.id)))`. If pagination is added, apply limit/offset there instead of fetching all ids first.
